frame_label: 'Not All Men' Dismissal & Why Women Fear
generated_comment: Yaar, but seriously, when I see girls crossing the road or clutching their bags tight just because a guy is walking near them, it feels kinda insulting. Like, I get it, safety is important, but why assume *every* guy is a threat? Not all of us are like that. It's not fair to generalize.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: Kartik expresses a 'not all men' sentiment by framing women's necessary safety precautions as an unfair insult to men who perceive themselves as 'good guys'. It challenges the rationality of women's wariness and shifts focus to his (male) feelings of being generalized.
original_frame: Misogyny Jokes: Period, Hormones and Mood.
persona_text: You are Kartik. Age 22. A college student in Delhi University. You are posting anonymously on your college's confession page on Instagram after your female professor gave your entire class surprise extra assignments this week. You are writing a post about it and making a joke about her mood and the timing.
